task_completion.description: Agent sends the required metric data points to Graphite Carbon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal window or script editor showing commands (like echo and nc/netcat) or code (like Python) sending data to localhost:2003 with the metric names 'apps.payment_service.response_time_ms', 'apps.payment_service.error_rate', and 'apps.payment_service.requests_per_sec'.

task_completion.description: Agent opens the Graphite web UI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a web browser opened to 'http://localhost/' displaying the Graphite web interface.

task_completion.description: Agent creates and names the dashboard 'Payment Service Health'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the dashboard title 'Payment Service Health' visible at the top of the Graphite dashboard interface.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the 'Response Time vs Errors' graph with the correct targets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph in the dashboard titled 'Response Time vs Errors'. The legend or target list must show 'apps.payment_service.response_time_ms' and 'apps.payment_service.error_rate'.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the 'Throughput' graph with the correct target.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph in the dashboard titled 'Throughput'. The legend or target list must show 'apps.payment_service.requests_per_sec'.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the 'Infrastructure Correlation' graph with the correct targets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph in the dashboard titled 'Infrastructure Correlation'. The legend or target list must show 'apps.payment_service.response_time_ms' and 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent is interacting with the actual Graphite web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the standard Graphite or Grafana UI elements (depending on the specific local setup), not a mock HTML page or text document.

integrity.description: Verify the graphs are actually rendering data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs should display plotted lines corresponding to the injected data points (e.g., a visible spike in response time and error rate), rather than being empty or displaying text placeholders.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't use browser developer tools to fake the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the browser's 'Inspect Element' or Developer Tools pane is not open and being used to manually edit the DOM to show the expected titles and metric names.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'Payment Service Health'. The custom metrics that must be visible in the graphs are 'apps.payment_service.response_time_ms', 'apps.payment_service.error_rate', and 'apps.payment_service.requests_per_sec'. The infrastructure metric to correlate with is 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization'.
